snippet.description:

Learn more here: tcgfhealthierlives.com

snippet.title: General Mills CEO talks growth outlook tied to cereals, dry pet food
snippet.description:
General Mills (GIS) reported better-than-expected first-quarter earnings, narrowly outpacing adjusted earnings per share and revenue while seeing net sales trend higher. General Mills Chairman and CEO Jeff Harmening joins Yahoo Finance Executive Editor Brian Sozzi to discuss value trends hitting the sales of Blue Buffalo pet products and its cereal brands. "In the short-term, it is clear that... pet parents are more mobile than they have been and going back to the office more. So they're treating less, little bit less wet pet food," Harmening says. "So, that was a drag on our sales. However, our dry pet food sales were up, so that's positive." Harmening goes on to talk about its innovation and prominence in its snacking and cereal categories. "We're always focused on market share because it's one of the things that we can control, and we've grown our market share in more than 50% of our categories for five years in a row. It's because we're focused on advertising," Harmening explains. Lastly, Harmening comments on acquisitions in the food space and how the Cheerios-maker is addressing inflation. For more expert insight and the latest market action, click here to watch this full episode of Yahoo Finance Live.
Subscribe to Yahoo Finance: https://yhoo.it/2fGu5Bb

snippet.title: Safety is Personal CEO Series: Doug L. Parker (Short Video Part 3)
snippet.description:
In her  Safety is Personal video series, NSC President and CEO Lorraine Martin asks safety professionals what got them into safety, and what keeps them there. In this short clip, Assistant Secretary of Labor for Occupational Safety and Health (OSHA) Doug L. Parker provides insight into what employers should focus on to provide safe workplaces.  

Visit NSC.org for safety tips and resources for the workplace and roadway.  

Find more #SafetyIsPersonal video conversations here: safetyispersonal.org.

#WorkplaceSafety #RoadwaySafety

snippet.title: Things You SHOULD Know About General Mills
snippet.description:
Discover the Untold History of General Mills - From Flour Mill to Global Food Giant | Did you know General Mills, the iconic packaged food company, had humble beginnings as a flour mill? In this video, we delve into the intriguing and lesser-known facts about General Mills' history, legacy, and achievements. From its early days as the ""Minneapolis Milling Company"" to becoming a leader in the breakfast cereal market, General Mills has been on a remarkable journey of diversification and adaptation.
Join us as we uncover surprising stories about General Mills' ventures into the toy-making, clothing, and restaurant industries, which led to the creation of iconic products like Play-Doh, Spirographs, and Red Lobster. We also explore how General Mills has evolved its food portfolio to include beloved brands like Cheerios, Pillsbury, Yoplait, and more.
Learn how General Mills has responded to changing consumer preferences by introducing healthier options and reducing artificial ingredients, all while maintaining its status as a global food conglomerate for over a hundred and fifty years.
Comment below with your thoughts on the most surprising facts from this video and your favorite General Mills product. Don't forget to hit the like button and subscribe for more captivating content on your favorite companies. Next time you shop for groceries, keep an eye out for the diverse range of General Mills products throughout the store - you'll be impressed!"
************
Check Out These Videos:
1. The Jim Henson Company - https://youtu.be/tYeTyOuLLUc
2. Southwest Airlines - https://youtu.be/jIkDCI91NBk
3. WinCo - https://youtu.be/0f0Mi7MlfRA

************
DISCLAIMER:  We're Not Journalists or Financial Advisors. We May Receive a Commission On Affiliate Links Used. All Content Is Used Within the Parameters of Fair Use. Please View our About Page to Contact us. This Video is Made For Entertainment Purposes Only.

snippet.title: Essex Property Trust Maintaining Capital Allocation Discipline
snippet.description:
Angela Kleiman, president and CEO of Essex Property Trust, Inc. (NYSE: ESS), participated in a video interview at Nareit’s REITweek: 2023 Investor Conference, held in New York June 6-8.

Kleiman discussed her priorities for the company in her new role as CEO, fundamentals in Essex’s core West Coast markets, including the supply outlook and whether Essex is seeing an impact on leasing levels from layoffs in the tech sector, how active Essex is likely to be on the acquisition, disposition, and development front for the remainder of 2023, and the outlook for the multifamily sector more broadly.
#REITsBolivia #REITs

snippet.title: CNBC Mad Money Interview Featuring David Cordani, Chairman and CEO of The Cigna Group
snippet.description:
After sharing our Q1 2023 results, David Cordani, chairman and CEO of The Cigna Group, joined CNBC’s Mad Money for an interview with host Jim Cramer. 
Watch the full interview below, which covered:   
Pharmacy Benefit Managers (PBMs): Overall growth in our business, our commitment to transparency, and positive outcomes for patients. 
Who we serve: Employers of all sizes, independent health plans, hospital systems, and government agencies. 
Biosimilars: Affordability and choice for patients and customers.

snippet.title: Mike Sievert CEO of T-Mobile – Taking Care of Business | T-Mobile for Business
snippet.description:
In this episode, Mike Katz sits down with the man himself T-Mobile CEO Mike Sievert! They discuss his philosophy on risk-taking, navigating the challenges of merging with Sprint, and becoming CEO during a pandemic.

00:00 Introduction
00:16 Mike Sievert's Background at T-Mobile
03:34 Adapting to IT Challenges
07:06 Adapting to Remote Work and Innovations
12:05 Risk-Taking Culture and Importance
17:31 Customer Influence on Company Decisions
20:34 Risk-Taking on People and Leadership
25:22 How to Navigate Challenges Embracing 5G Innovation
32:48 Future of 5G and T-Mobile's Leadership Role
